title: Create Your First Agent
lede: In the next few minutes you'll build an AI voice agent that can answer calls, hold natural conversations, and work for you 24/7 — no code, no waiting. Here's the full journey, from a blank list to a talking agent.

steps.title: Open Smart Agents
steps.content:
Every agent you build lives in one place: the Smart Agents list.

  1. In the header, click the Automation section (it's usually the first tab).
  2. Click the Smart Agents sub-tab — or use the left sidebar: under Intelligence Center, click Smart Agents.
  3. You'll see a table of your agents with their Voice, LLM, STT (transcriber), TTS (voice engine), and Label columns.

The toolbar above the table gives you:

  • Label filter — narrow the list to agents tagged with a label.
  • Owner filter — filter by main account or a sub-account owner.
  • List / Folders — switch between a flat table and a folder (label-grouped) view.
  • Create New Agent — the blue button we're about to press.
  • API Info — shows how to create and manage agents programmatically via the API.

images.alt: Smart Agents list with the Create New Agent button
images.caption: The Smart Agents list — every agent you build lives here.

images.alt: Smart Agents list with the Create New Agent button
images.caption: The Smart Agents list — every agent you build lives here.

steps.title: Click Create New Agent
steps.content:
1. Click Create New Agent in the top-right of the toolbar.
  2. That's it — iMash instantly creates a ready-to-run agent (named something like Agent_1234567) with a working default brain, voice, and transcriber, and opens it in the editor.

You never start from a broken blank slate: a brand-new agent can already talk. Everything you do from here is customization.

💡 Tip: The small arrow next to Create New Agent opens a menu with Browse Templates — a faster start if a pre-built agent fits your use case (next step).

images.alt: Toolbar with the Create New Agent button
images.caption: The toolbar: filters, view toggle, and the Create New Agent button.

images.alt: Toolbar with the Create New Agent button
images.caption: The toolbar: filters, view toggle, and the Create New Agent button.

steps.title: Optional: Start From a Template
steps.content:
Don't want to write a prompt from scratch? Click the arrow next to Create New Agent and choose Browse Templates.

  • System Templates — professionally written agents for common jobs: After-Hours Answering, FAQ & Info Line, Lead Follow-Up, Appointment Reminder, Survey & Feedback, Technical Support, and more.
  • Organization Templates — templates shared inside your organization.
  • My Templates — agents you've saved with Save As (covered later in this guide).
  1. Use the search box or the category chips (Inbound, Outbound, Support, Sales, Scheduling, General) to find a fit.
  2. Click Use Template on a card — a new agent is created from it and opens in the editor, ready to personalize.

images.alt: Agent Templates gallery
images.caption: The Agent Templates gallery — one click creates a fully configured agent.

images.alt: Agent Templates gallery
images.caption: The Agent Templates gallery — one click creates a fully configured agent.

steps.title: Meet the Editor: the Node Canvas
steps.content:
New agents open in the Node view — a visual canvas where your agent is a living diagram. Everything on screen is real configuration, not decoration:

  • The Agent node (center-right) — your agent's heart: its inputs, identity, capabilities, and behavior.
  • Provider nodes wired into it — an LLM node (the brain and its model + temperature), a TTS node (the voice it speaks with), and an STT node (how it transcribes what callers say). The animated dotted lines show what feeds what.
  • Capabilities bar (top) — ON/OFF cards for Knowledge Base, Webhooks, Observer Agent, IVR, CRM, and more (step 7).
  • Action rail (right edge) — Components palette, Call logs, Version history, Share, Editor settings, and theme toggle.
  • Agent Builder bar (bottom) — talk to an AI that builds the canvas for you, or live-test your agent (step 9).
  • Navigation pill (bottom-left) — Back, the Form / Node view toggle, owner switcher, and Save As (template).
💡 Tip: Everything autosaves. Watch the badge in the bottom-right corner next to the agent name — it flips to Saved · just now after each change. There is no Save button to forget.

images.alt: The node canvas editor with agent and provider nodes
images.caption: The Node view: your agent as a live diagram — brain, voice, and ears wired into the Agent node.

images.alt: The node canvas editor with agent and provider nodes
images.caption: The Node view: your agent as a live diagram — brain, voice, and ears wired into the Agent node.

steps.title: Name the Agent and Write Its Persona
steps.content:
Time to give your agent a personality. On the Agent node, click the gear icon (top-right corner of the node) to open the Agent Identity panel:

  • Agent name — replace the auto-generated name with something you'll recognize, e.g. Acme Receptionist.
  • Persona — the system prompt. Describe in plain language who the agent is, what it must do, its tone, and its rules. This is the single most important field in the whole editor.
  • Agent language — the language(s) the agent listens in (e.g. Multilingual, English, Spanish).
  • Conversation → How the call opens — who speaks first and the exact greeting. Choose whether the caller speaks first, the AI improvises an opener, or the AI says a defined message you write word-for-word.
💡 Prompt tips: Write the persona like you'd brief a new employee — role, goal, tone, do's and don'ts. Keep it under ~3,000 tokens (the editor shows an estimate); move long reference material into the Knowledge Base instead.

images.alt: Agent Identity panel with name, persona, and language
images.caption: Agent Identity: name, persona (system prompt), language, and how the call opens.

images.alt: Agent Identity panel with name, persona, and language
images.caption: Agent Identity: name, persona (system prompt), language, and how the call opens.

steps.title: Check the Brain, Voice, and Ears
steps.content:
Look at the Agent node's INPUTS section: LLM, TTS, and STT each connect to a provider node on the canvas.

  1. On the LLM node, click the Model field to pick the model that thinks up replies, and drag the Temperature slider (Precise ↔ Creative).
  2. On the TTS node, click the Voice field to choose the voice your agent speaks with (expand Advanced for fine-tuning).
  3. On the STT node, review the transcription Model and Language, and optionally add Keyword Boost terms (brand names the transcriber should recognize).
  4. To swap a provider entirely, click the gear icon on any provider node.

The defaults are production-ready, so you can skip this step on day one — deep dives live in the Choose the Brain (LLM) and Voice Configuration guides.

images.alt: The Agent node with LLM, TTS, and STT inputs
images.caption: The Agent node: inputs (LLM, TTS, STT), identity, capabilities, and behavior at a glance.

images.alt: The Agent node with LLM, TTS, and STT inputs
images.caption: The Agent node: inputs (LLM, TTS, STT), identity, capabilities, and behavior at a glance.

steps.title: Switch On Capabilities
steps.content:
The bar across the top of the canvas is your agent's superpower switchboard. Each card mirrors a full settings panel — click a card to configure it, or flip its ON/OFF pill to enable it. When a capability is ON, an animated line connects it to your agent.

  • Settings — call timeouts, idle messages, voicemail behavior, privacy & recording.
  • Knowledge Base — let the agent answer from your uploaded documents (RAG).
  • Webhooks — post-call data extraction and webhook delivery.
  • Observer Agent — a second AI that watches live calls and flags/extracts in real time.
  • IVR — phone-menu navigation behavior.
  • CRM / Dispatch — CRM intelligence and field-service dispatch powers.
  • Messages — run this same agent over SMS, WhatsApp, Email, and web chat.
  • Widget — embed the agent on your website.
💡 Tip: The leftmost fold button collapses the bar into a single gear icon when you want a clean canvas — a green badge shows how many capabilities are ON.

images.alt: Capabilities bar with ON/OFF toggle cards
images.caption: The capabilities bar — every card opens a full settings panel; pills flip features ON/OFF.

images.alt: Capabilities bar with ON/OFF toggle cards
images.caption: The capabilities bar — every card opens a full settings panel; pills flip features ON/OFF.

steps.title: Explore the Components Palette
steps.content:
Ready to go beyond a single agent? The action rail on the right edge of the canvas holds the power tools:

  1. Click the grid icon (top of the rail) to open the Components panel.
  2. Drag any component onto the canvas to add it:
  • Components — extra LLM, TTS, STT, or Agent nodes.
  • Agents & Transitions — Task (a scoped sub-call with its own prompt), If / Else and Category (decision nodes that branch the conversation), Instruction Agent, and Handoff (transfer to another agent). This is how one agent becomes a whole team — see the Sub-Agents, Tasks & Handoffs guide.
  • Tools — End Call, SIP Transfer, Internal Transfer, and more actions the agent can take mid-call.

The rest of the rail: Call logs for this agent, Version history (restore any earlier canvas), Share (copy a deep link), Editor settings (grid, snap, auto-layout), and a theme toggle.

images.alt: Components palette open over the canvas
images.caption: The Components palette: drag tasks, decision nodes, handoffs, and tools straight onto the canvas.

images.alt: Components palette open over the canvas
images.caption: The Components palette: drag tasks, decision nodes, handoffs, and tools straight onto the canvas.

steps.title: Build With AI, Then Talk to Your Agent
steps.content:
The bar at the bottom of the canvas is where the magic happens — it has two modes:

  • AI Builder — press Start (or type in the box below) and literally tell the AI what to build: "Add an If/Else that branches on caller intent" or "Create a Categorize node for billing / support / sales". It edits the canvas for you in real time.
  • Live Test — switch the toggle and press Start to place a live voice call to the agent exactly as configured on the canvas. Your browser will ask for microphone permission — allow it, then just talk.
  1. Click Live Test, then Start.
  2. Say hello and check the greeting, the voice, and the persona.
  3. Tweak, test again, repeat — changes autosave, so every test uses your latest edits.
⚠️ Heads up: Test calls use real speech providers, so make sure your account has credit. If the agent doesn't answer, check that the LLM, Voice, and Transcriber are all enabled (their pills show red when a critical piece is off).

images.alt: AI Builder and Live Test bar with the composer input
images.caption: AI Builder edits the canvas from plain English; Live Test calls your agent right in the browser.

images.alt: AI Builder and Live Test bar with the composer input
images.caption: AI Builder edits the canvas from plain English; Live Test calls your agent right in the browser.

steps.title: Prefer Classic Forms? Toggle the Form View
steps.content:
The canvas and the classic form editor are two windows onto the same agent — switch any time, nothing is lost.

  1. In the bottom-left navigation pill, click Form on the Form / Node toggle.
  2. The editor becomes a three-column layout: the system prompt and greeting on the left, the feature panels (Knowledge Base, Transcriber, Voice, Brains, Webhooks, Observer, IVR, CRM Intelligence, Messages, Website Widget, Templates & Variables) in the middle, and the Test Agent chat on the right.
  3. Click Node to jump back to the canvas.

The form-view header also shows the agent's ID (one-click copy — you'll need it for API calls), an Est. tokens counter for your prompt, the Owner selector, and status pills that go green when Voice, Transcriber, and LLM are ready.

images.alt: Form view with prompt, feature panels, and test chat
images.caption: Form view: prompt on the left, feature panels in the middle, live test chat on the right.

images.alt: Form view with prompt, feature panels, and test chat
images.caption: Form view: prompt on the left, feature panels in the middle, live test chat on the right.

images.alt: Form view header toolbar
images.caption: The form-view header: name, ID, token estimate, owner, Form/Node toggle, Save As, and Test Agent.

images.alt: Form view header toolbar
images.caption: The form-view header: name, ID, token estimate, owner, Form/Node toggle, Save As, and Test Agent.

steps.title: Save as a Template and Go Live
steps.content:
Your agent is alive — here's how to protect and launch it:

  • Autosave — every edit is already persisted; the bottom-right badge confirms it (Saved · just now).
  • Save As (bottom-left pill in Node view, header button in Form view) — snapshot the agent as a reusable template so your team can spin up copies from Browse Templates → My Templates.
  • Version history (clock icon on the action rail) — restore any earlier version if an experiment goes sideways.
  • Go live — attach the agent to a phone number so it answers real calls, or embed it on your website. That's the payoff: a tireless agent that never misses a call.

images.alt: Bottom-left navigation pill with Form/Node toggle and Save As
images.caption: The navigation pill: Back, Form/Node toggle, owner switcher, and Save As (template).

images.alt: Bottom-left navigation pill with Form/Node toggle and Save As
images.caption: The navigation pill: Back, Form/Node toggle, owner switcher, and Save As (template).

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaTitle: Create Your First Agent | iMash Documentation
metaDescription: Step-by-step guide to creating an iMash Smart Agent: create it in one click or from a template, master the node canvas and form view, write the persona, pick the brain and voice, switch on capabilities, and live-test it in the browser.
metaKeywords: create agent, imash, smart agent, voice agent, ai assistant, node editor, canvas, agent template, persona, system prompt, ai builder, live test
